concerns.text: FR-006 defines the negative‑sampling strategy as “sample a set of gene‑pair negatives equal in size to the positive set, sampled uniformly from all possible gene pairs not present in STRING”. Using absence in STRING as negatives is not an independent ground‑truth; many true PPIs are simply unrecorded, so the validation target is not truly independent of the predictor and may inflate performance.

FR-006 also states that AUROC/AUPRC are computed on the sampled, size‑balanced positive and negative sets. AUROC assumes a ranking over the full population; evaluating on a balanced subset can bias the metric and does not reflect real‑world class imbalance, potentially leading to over‑optimistic success criteria (SC-001).
